---
title: Sync Code Generation
description: Maintain generated sync clients from async source modules.
---

The async modules are the source of truth for most endpoint, client,
middleware, and service implementations. The sync modules listed in
`scripts/gen_sync.py` are generated from those async sources.

Generated files start with:

```python
# Generated by scripts/gen_sync.py from src/ksef2/clients/async_auth.py.
# DO NOT EDIT BY HAND - edit the async source and run `just gen-sync`.
```

## Change a generated pair

1. Edit the async source module.

   ```bash
   $EDITOR src/ksef2/clients/async_auth.py
   ```

2. Generate the matching sync module.

   ```bash
   just gen-sync
   ```

   For a focused pass, use the generator directly:

   ```bash
   uv run python scripts/gen_sync.py --only src/ksef2/clients/async_auth.py
   ```

3. Review both the async source and generated sync target.

   ```bash
   git diff -- src/ksef2/clients/async_auth.py src/ksef2/clients/auth.py
   ```

4. Run the generated-sync check before opening a PR.

   ```bash
   just check-gen-sync
   ```

`just release-check` also runs `check-gen-sync`, so stale generated sync files
block releases.

## Check one pair

Use `--only` with either the async source or sync target:

```bash
uv run python scripts/gen_sync.py --check --diff --only src/ksef2/clients/auth.py
```

This is useful when you are iterating on one generated pair and want a compact
diff.

## Know the boundaries

Not every sync/async pair is generated. Some modules are intentionally shared or
structurally different:

- `src/ksef2/core/polling.py` contains shared sync and async polling helpers.
- `src/ksef2/core/response_errors.py` contains shared response error mapping.
- `src/ksef2/clients/base.py` and `src/ksef2/clients/async_base.py` differ in
  root-client ownership and lifecycle.
- `src/ksef2/core/http.py` and `src/ksef2/core/async_http.py` differ in
  transport ownership and close semantics.

The generator keeps the explicit pair list and exclusions in `scripts/gen_sync.py`.
Update that list when a module becomes generated or intentionally divergent.

## What the release gate proves

`just release-check` runs:

```bash
just lint
just format-check
just check-ksef-api-version
just check-gen-sync
just typecheck
just test
just test-runtime-checks
uv build
```

For sync generation specifically, that means:

- generated targets match the async source transform;
- generated files still format with the repo config;
- sync and async public surfaces stay covered by parity tests;
- the package builds from the checked-in generated files.
